metadata.title: Brave Irene
metadata.description: A story about a brave and loving girl named Irene, who braves a fierce winter storm to deliver a beautiful ball gown made by her sick mother to the duchess.

sentences.englishOriginal: Hi, I'm Al Gore and this is part of the BookPALS program, sponsored by the Screen Actors Guild Foundation.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 嗨，我是高爾，這是由螢幕演員公會基金會所贊助的 BookPALS 計畫的一部分。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Hello, my name is Al Gore, and this segment is part of the BookPALS initiative, which is proudly sponsored by the Screen Actors Guild Foundation.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 資助、贊助。此處為過去分詞作形容詞修飾 program。

grammarNotes.explanation: 過去分詞短語作後置修飾語，修飾前面的名詞 program。

sentences.englishOriginal: And today, I'm going to read you this book, written and illustrated by William Steig, it's called 'Brave Irene.'
sentences.chineseTranslation: 今天，我要讀這本由威廉·史代格寫作並繪製插畫的書給你們聽，書名叫作《勇敢的艾琳》。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Today, I will be reading a book titled 'Brave Irene,' written and illustrated by William Steig.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: （為書等）畫插圖。與 written 並列修飾 book。

sentences.englishOriginal: Here's how it begins...
sentences.chineseTranslation: 故事是這樣開始的……
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: This is how the story unfolds...

phraseNotes.explanation: 這就是……的方式。常用於引導或開始敘述某事。

sentences.englishOriginal: Mrs. Bobbin, the dressmaker, was tired and had a bad headache, but she still managed to sew the last stitches in the gown she was making.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 裁縫師巴賓太太感到很疲倦且頭痛得厲害，但她還是設法在她正在製作的禮服上縫上了最後幾針。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Despite being exhausted and suffering from a severe headache, Mrs. Bobbin, the dressmaker, managed to put the final stitches into the gown she was working on.

phraseNotes.explanation: 設法做到某事、勉強完成某件困難的事。

grammarNotes.explanation: 省略了關係代名詞 which/that 的定語從句，修飾前面的 the gown。

sentences.englishOriginal: It's the most beautiful dress in the whole world! said her daughter, Irene. The duchess will love it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這是全世界最漂亮的洋裝了！」她的女兒艾琳說。「公爵夫人一定會喜歡的。」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'This is the most gorgeous gown in the entire world!' exclaimed her daughter, Irene. 'The duchess is going to adore it.'

grammarNotes.explanation: 最高級形容詞用法，修飾單數可數名詞 dress。

sentences.englishOriginal: It is nice, her mother admitted. But, dumpling, it's for tonight's ball, and I don't have the strength to bring it. I feel sick.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「是很漂亮，」她母親承認道。「但是，寶貝，這是為了今晚的舞會準備的，而我現在沒力氣送過去了。我病倒了。」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'It does look lovely,' her mother acknowledged. 'However, sweetheart, it is meant for this evening's ball, and I lack the energy to deliver it. I feel quite ill.'

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 本意為水餃、團子，在此作為對孩子的親暱稱呼（小寶貝、小心肝）。

phraseNotes.phrase: have the strength to do sth
phraseNotes.explanation: 有力氣或精力去做某事。

nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 英語文化中常用各種甜點食物來作為對家人、愛人或孩子的親暱稱呼（Terms of endearment）。

sentences.englishOriginal: Poor Mama, said Irene. I can get it there!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「可憐的媽媽，」艾琳說。「我可以把它送過去！」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Poor Mommy,' Irene said. 'I can take it over there for you!'

phraseNotes.explanation: 把東西送到目的地。

sentences.englishOriginal: No, cupcake, I can't let you, said Mrs. Bobbin.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「不行，小甜心，我不能讓你去，」巴賓太太說。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'No, sweetheart, I cannot allow you to do that,' Mrs. Bobbin replied.

phraseNotes.explanation: 讓某人做某事，let 為使役動詞，後接不帶 to 的動詞原型。

sentences.englishOriginal: Such a huge package, and it's such a long way to the palace. Besides, it's starting to snow.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「那麼大一個包裹，而且到皇宮的路又那麼遠。更何況，天已經開始下雪了。」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 「It is such a massive parcel, and the palace is a great distance away. Furthermore, it is beginning to snow.」

phraseNotes.explanation: 如此……的一個東西，用以加強語氣。
去往某處的很長一段路程。

sentences.englishOriginal: But I love snow, Irene insisted.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「可是我最喜歡雪了，」艾琳堅持著說。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'But I absolutely adore the snow,' Irene maintained.

sentences.englishOriginal: She coaxed her mother into bed, covered her with two quilts, and added a blanket for her feet.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她哄著母親上床躺下，幫她蓋了兩條被子，又在她的腳邊加了一條毛毯。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She gently persuaded her mother into bed, tucked her in with two heavy quilts, and placed an extra blanket over her feet.

phraseNotes.explanation: 耐心地哄某人做某事或進入某個狀態。

sentences.englishOriginal: Then she fixed some tea with lemon and honey and put more wood in the stove.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 接著，她準備了一些加了檸檬和蜂蜜的茶，並在爐子裡添了更多木柴。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Afterward, she prepared some tea mixed with lemon and honey, and added extra wood to the stove.

phraseNotes.explanation: 此處的 fix 當作「準備、泡（茶/咖啡）」或「做（飯）」解，是非常道地的口語說法。

sentences.englishOriginal: With great care, Irene took the splendid gown down from the dummy and packed it in a big box with plenty of tissue paper.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳非常小心地將這件無與倫比的華麗禮服從人體模特兒上取下來，並用大量的包裝薄紙將它裝進一個大箱子裡。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: With immense caution, Irene removed the gorgeous gown from the mannequin and packed it into a large box with a generous amount of tissue paper.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: （裁縫或服裝店用的）人體模型。
在此多指包裝精品或衣服時用來防皺、保護的「包裝薄紙」，而非單指衛生紙。

phraseNotes.explanation: 非常小心、極其謹慎地。
大量的、充足的（後可接可數或不可數名詞）。

sentences.englishOriginal: Dress warmly, pudding, her mother called in a weak voice, and don't forget to button up. It's cold out there, and windy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「衣服穿暖一點，小寶貝，」母親用虛弱的聲音喊著，「還有別忘了把扣子扣好。外頭又冷風又大。」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Bundle up warmly, sweetheart,' her mother called out weakly, 'and make sure to button up your coat. It is freezing and quite windy outside.'

phraseNotes.explanation: 穿得暖和、穿厚一點。

sentences.englishOriginal: Irene put on her fleece-lined boots, her red hat and muffler, her heavy coat, and her mittens.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳穿上了她刷毛的靴子、戴上她的紅帽子和圍巾、穿上厚重的大衣並戴上手套。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Irene pulled on her fleece-lined boots, donned her red winter hat and scarf, and put on her thick coat along with her mittens.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 內裡刷毛的、內襯羊羔絨的。
厚圍巾（較傳統的用詞，現多用 scarf）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 穿上、戴上（衣服、配件等）。

sentences.englishOriginal: She kissed her mother's hot forehead six times, then once again, made sure she was tucked in snugly,
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她在母親發燙的額頭上吻了六下，接著又吻了一次，確保母親被子蓋得嚴嚴實實、非常舒適，
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She planted six kisses on her mother's feverish forehead, followed by one last one, ensured she was cozily tucked in,

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 溫暖舒適地、緊湊貼合地。

phraseNotes.explanation: 幫某人蓋好被子安頓睡覺。

sentences.englishOriginal: and slipped out with the big box, shutting the door firmly behind her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後抱著那個大箱子悄悄溜了出去，並在她身後牢牢地把門關上。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: and slipped outside carrying the massive box, locking or closing the door securely behind her.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 悄悄溜出、順暢地閃身出去（過去式）。

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在分詞短語作伴隨狀語，表示出門時隨手把門關緊。

sentences.englishOriginal: It really was cold outside, very cold.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 外面真的非常冷，冷得不得了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The weather outside was genuinely freezing, exceptionally cold.

sentences.englishOriginal: The wind whirled the falling snow-flakes about, this way, that way, and into Irene's squinting face.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 狂風將落下的雪花到處吹散，一下子吹向這邊，一下子吹向那邊，迎面直撲向艾琳那瞇著的雙眼。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The gale whipped the tumbling snowflakes around in every direction, blowing them right into Irene's squinting face.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 瞇著眼看的。因為風雪大而不得不把眼睛瞇起來。

phraseNotes.explanation: 忽左忽右、到處、四面八方。

sentences.englishOriginal: She set out on the uphill path to Farmer Bennett's sheep pasture.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她踏上了前往農夫班奈特家牧羊場的上坡路。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She started hiking along the ascending trail leading toward Farmer Bennett's sheep meadow.

phraseNotes.explanation: 出發前往、著手進行（一段旅程或計畫）。

sentences.englishOriginal: By the time she got there, the snow was up to her ankles and the wind was worse.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當她到達那裡時，積雪已經沒過她的腳踝，而且風勢變得更加猛烈了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: By the moment she reached the pasture, the snow had accumulated up to her ankles, and the wind had intensified.

phraseNotes.explanation: 到了……的時候（引導時間副詞從句）。
高達、深至、達到……的程度。

sentences.englishOriginal: It hurried her along and made her stumble.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 風催促著她前進，並讓她不斷跌跌撞撞。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The powerful gusts pushed her forward aggressively, causing her to lose her footing and trip.

phraseNotes.explanation: 催促或強迫某人走得更快。

sentences.englishOriginal: Irene resented this; the box was problem enough.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳對此感到十分怨恨；光是抱著這個大箱子就已經夠讓她頭痛的了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Irene felt quite irritated by this interference, as managing the large box was already a significant challenge.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 對……感到憤恨、怨恨（過去式）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 本身就已經夠是個麻煩/問題了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Easy does it! she cautioned the wind, leaning back hard against it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「別亂來！小心點！」她一邊警告狂風，一邊用力往後傾身體來頂住強風。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Take it easy!' she warned the gale, as she leaned her entire body backward to resist its force.

phraseNotes.explanation: 口語常用片語，意思是「慢慢來、小心點、別太衝動」。
斜靠著……、頂著……。

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在分詞短語作伴隨狀語，描述她警告風時的肢體動作。

sentences.englishOriginal: By the middle of the pasture, the flakes were falling thicker.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 走到牧場中央時，雪花落得更密、更厚了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: By the time she reached the center of the field, the snow was falling with much greater density.

sentences.englishOriginal: Now the wind drove Irene along so rudely she had to hop, skip, and go helter-skeltering over the knobby ground.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這時，狂風極其粗暴地推著艾琳往前衝，她不得不連蹦帶跳、在崎嶇不平的地面上慌亂地跌撞奔逃。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: At this point, the wind propelled Irene forward so violently that she was forced to leap, stumble, and rush wildly across the bumpy terrain.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 慌慌張張地、狼狽奔跑地。
多節的、疙瘩的、在此形容地面「崎嶇不平」。

phraseNotes.explanation: 驅趕、推著某人前進。

grammarNotes.explanation: so... that...（如此……以至於……）句型，因 that 之後是接續動作，此處常省略關係詞 directly。

sentences.englishOriginal: Cold snow sifted into her boots and chilled her feet.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 冰冷的雪花滲進了她的靴子裡，凍得她雙腳發麻。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Freezing snow filtered into her footwear, making her feet feel incredibly cold.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 篩、滲透、緩緩飄落（過去式）。

sentences.englishOriginal: She pushed out her lip and hurried on. This was an important errand.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她抿起嘴唇（噘起嘴表示決心），繼續加快腳步。因為這是一趟至關重要的差事。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Pouting with determination, she accelerated her pace, knowing this mission was of great significance.

phraseNotes.explanation: 噘嘴。在文學中常形容小孩不服輸、鬧彆扭或下定決心的倔強神情。
急忙繼續前進。

stories.title: The Disaster and the Lost Dress

sentences.englishOriginal: When she reached Apple Road, the wind decided to put on a show.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當她走到蘋果路時，這陣風決定要好好大顯身手一番。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Upon her arrival at Apple Road, the gale seemed determined to display its full and destructive power.

phraseNotes.explanation: 大顯身手、上演一場表演（此處是擬人化，形容風勢開始大作）。

sentences.englishOriginal: It ripped branches from trees and flung them about, swept up and scattered the fallen snow,
sentences.chineseTranslation: 它把樹枝從樹上狠狠扯下並到處亂扔，將地上的積雪捲起又漫天散落，
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: It tore limbs off the trees, tossing them around wildly, while lifting and dispersing the accumulated snow,

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 猛投、拋擲（fling 的過去式）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 席捲而起、掃起來。

sentences.englishOriginal: got in front of Irene to keep her from moving ahead.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 甚至擋在艾琳面前，阻止她繼續向前邁進。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: and blocked Irene's path directly to prevent her from making any forward progress.

phraseNotes.explanation: 阻止/防止某人做某事。
向前推進、前進。

sentences.englishOriginal: Irene turned around and pressed on backwards.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳索性轉過身來，背對著風倒退著艱難前進。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Irene turned her back to the storm and stubbornly forced her way forward by walking backward.

phraseNotes.explanation: 不顧艱難地繼續前進。
向後地、倒退地。

sentences.englishOriginal: Go home! the wind squalled. Irene... go hooome...
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「回家吧！」風狂吼著。「艾琳……快回——家——吧——」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Go back home!' the gale roared. 'Irene... return hooomme...'

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 狂風大作、尖叫、大聲啼哭（過去式）。

sentences.englishOriginal: I will do no such thing, she snapped. No such thing, you wicked wind!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我才不幹呢，」她厲聲反駁。「門都沒有，你這可惡的惡風！」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'I will absolutely not do that,' she countered sharply. 'Not a chance, you evil wind!'

vocabularyNotes.explanation: （厲聲）斥責、啪一聲折斷（過去式）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 絕不做這樣的事、絕不答應。

sentences.englishOriginal: Go ho-o-ome, the wind yodeled. GO HO-WO-WOME, it shrieked, or else.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「快回——家——」風怪叫著。「快——回——家——！」它尖叫著，「不然要你好看！」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Go ho-o-ome,' the storm sang out. 'GO HO-WO-WOME,' it screamed, 'or you will regret it.'

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 用約德爾唱法唱歌、此處形容風聲高低起伏的怪叫聲（過去式）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 否則、不然的話（通常帶有強烈的威脅語氣）。

sentences.englishOriginal: For a short second, Irene wondered if she shouldn't heed the wind's warning.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 有那麼短短一秒鐘，艾琳懷疑自己是不是真的應該聽從風的警告。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: For a brief moment, Irene contemplated whether she ought to listen to the warning of the gale.

grammarNotes.explanation: if 在此引導賓語從句，表示「是否……」。

sentences.englishOriginal: But no! The gown had to get to the duchess!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但不行！這件禮服一定要送到公爵夫人手中！
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: But she refused! The ball gown absolutely had to be delivered to the duchess!

phraseNotes.explanation: 必須、不得不。

sentences.englishOriginal: The wind wrestled her for the package-- walloped it, twisted it, shook it, snatched at it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 風開始和她爭奪那個包裹——猛烈敲打它、扭曲它、劇烈搖晃它、還試圖一把抓走它。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The storm fought her for the parcel—battering, twisting, shaking, and clawing at it viciously.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 猛擊、痛打（過去式，文學修辭用法）。
伸手猛抓、企圖一把奪取（過去式）。

sentences.englishOriginal: But Irene wouldn't yield. It's my mother's work! she screamed.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但艾琳絕不屈服。「這是我媽媽辛辛苦苦做的！」她尖叫著。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Yet Irene refused to give up. 'This is my mother's hard work!' she shrieked.

sentences.englishOriginal: Then-- oh, woe!-- the box was wrenched from her mittened grasp and sent bumbling along in the snow.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 接著——天啊，悲劇發生了！——箱子從她戴著手套的雙手中被狠狠奪走，在雪地上沒命似地滾動開來。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Then—alas!—the box was violently torn from her mittened hands and went tumbling away across the snow.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 悲哀、苦難。oh, woe! 常用來感嘆「天啊！悲劇啊！」
猛扭、猛拉、強行奪走（過去式）。
在此形容笨拙地翻滾、跌跌撞撞地移動。

grammarNotes.explanation: 被動語態的並列結構，主詞是 the box。

sentences.englishOriginal: Irene went after it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳立刻追了過去。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Irene immediately chased after it.

phraseNotes.explanation: 追趕、追求。

sentences.englishOriginal: She pounced and took hold, but the ill-tempered wind ripped the box open.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她猛撲過去抓住了箱子，但那壞脾氣的惡風卻一把將箱子給扯開了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She dove and managed to grab it, but the malicious gale tore the package open.

phraseNotes.explanation: 抓住、固定住、起作用。
把某物猛烈撕開。

sentences.englishOriginal: The ball gown flounced out and went waltzing through the powdered air with tissue-paper attendants.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 舞會禮服蓬地一聲飛了出來，伴隨著那些包裝薄紙隨從，在漫天如粉末般的風雪中翩翩起舞。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The evening dress burst out and danced gracefully through the snowy air, accompanied by drifting pieces of tissue paper.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: （大動作地）擺動、蹦出、扭動（過去式）。
跳華爾滋舞。此處精妙地形容衣服在空中飛舞的姿態。
成粉末狀的。此處形容像粉末一樣細密的雪景。
隨從、服務員（複數）。此處把圍繞禮服飛散的包裝紙擬人化為隨從。

sentences.englishOriginal: Irene clung to the empty box and watched the beautiful gown disappear.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳緊緊抱著空空如也的箱子，眼睜睜看著那件美麗的禮服消失在風雪深處。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Holding fast to the empty carton, Irene helplessly watched the gorgeous dress vanish from sight.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 緊抓、依附（cling 的過去式）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 緊緊抱住、堅守。

sentences.englishOriginal: How could anything so terribly wrong be allowed to happen? Tears froze on her lashes.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 怎麼能讓如此糟糕、如此不公的事情發生呢？淚水在她的睫毛上結成了冰。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: How could such a devastating misfortune be permitted to occur? Her tears turned to ice on her eyelashes.

grammarNotes.explanation: 被動語態，allow 加上不訂式，意為「被允許發生」。

sentences.englishOriginal: Her dear mother's hard work, all those days of measuring, cutting, pinning, stitching... for this?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她親愛的媽媽付出的一切心血，那整整好幾天親自量身、剪裁、別針固定、細心縫紉……難道就落得這種下場嗎？
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: All the dedication of her beloved mother—days spent measuring, tailoring, pinning, and sewing—had it all been for nothing?

phraseNotes.explanation: 就為了這個？（常用於表達極度失望、無奈或憤慨）。

grammarNotes.explanation: 連續動名詞並列，用作介系詞 of 的受詞，展現精細的服裝製作工序。

stories.title: Injured and Lost in the Dark

sentences.englishOriginal: And the poor duchess! Irene decided she would have to trudge on with just the box and explain everything in person.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 還有那可憐的公爵夫人！艾琳決定，哪怕只抱著一個空箱子，她也必須一步一腳印地往前走，親自去向公爵夫人登門致歉並解釋清楚。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: And what about the unfortunate duchess! Irene resolved that she must push forward carrying only the empty container to explain the situation personally.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 步履艱難地走、沉重地緩步前進。

phraseNotes.explanation: 親自、本人。

sentences.englishOriginal: She went shuffling through the snow. Would her mother understand, she wondered, that it was the wind's fault, not hers?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她拖著沉重的步伐在雪地中蹣跚前行。她心裡直犯咕嘡：媽媽會理解這全是風的錯，而不是因為她粗心大意嗎？
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She dragged her feet through the deep drifts. She questioned within herself whether her mother would comprehend that the disaster was entirely caused by the storm, not herself.

phraseNotes.explanation: 某人的錯、某人的責任。

grammarNotes.structure: Would her mother understand... that it was...
grammarNotes.explanation: that 引導名詞從句，作為 understand 的受詞。

sentences.englishOriginal: Would the duchess be angry? The wind was howling like a wild animal.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 公爵夫人會大發雷霆嗎？此時，風像一頭被激怒的野獸般瘋狂咆哮著。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Would the duchess react with fury? The gale was roaring like a savage beast.

phraseNotes.explanation: 像野獸一般（明喻修辭）。

sentences.englishOriginal: Suddenly Irene stepped in a hole and fell over with a twisted ankle. She blamed it on the wind.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 突然間，艾琳一腳踩進了一個隱蔽的雪坑裡，狠狠摔了一跤，當場扭傷了腳踝。她把這筆帳全算在了這陣惡風的頭上。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: All of a sudden, Irene stepped into a hidden depression and collapsed, spraining her ankle. She held the wind responsible.

phraseNotes.explanation: 將某事歸咎於、怪罪於某人或某物。

sentences.englishOriginal: Keep quiet! She scolded. You've done enough damage already. You've spoiled everything! Everything!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「閉嘴！」她厲聲訓斥道。「你造成的破壞已經夠多了。你把一切都給毀了！全部都給毀了！」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Shut up!' she yelled angrily. 'You have already caused far too much destruction. You have ruined absolutely everything!'

phraseNotes.explanation: 保持安靜！閉嘴！

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在完成式，強調過去發生的動作對現在造成了無法挽回的結果。

sentences.englishOriginal: The wind swallowed up her words.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然而，呼嘯的狂風瞬間就將她的聲音吞沒得無影無蹤。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Her angry shouts were completely drowned out by the roaring storm.

phraseNotes.explanation: 吞沒、耗盡、淹沒。

sentences.englishOriginal: She sat in the snow in great pain, afraid she wouldn't be able to go on.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她癱坐在雪地上，強烈的劇痛襲來，她好害怕自己再也無法挪動半步了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She remained seated in the deep snow, agonizing in pain, terrified that she could no longer continue her journey.

phraseNotes.explanation: 能夠、有能力去做……
繼續前進、繼續下去。

grammarNotes.explanation: 形容詞 afraid 後面省略了連接詞 that 引導的形容詞補語從句。

sentences.englishOriginal: But she managed to get to her feet and start moving. It hurt.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但她最終還是咬緊牙關，掙扎著站了起來，再度開始邁步。每走一步都痛徹心扉。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Nevertheless, she somehow succeeded in standing up and resuming her progress, despite the severe agony.

phraseNotes.explanation: 站起身來。

sentences.englishOriginal: Home, where she longed to be, where she and her mother could be warm together, was far behind.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 家，那個她無比渴望回去、那個能和媽媽靠在一起依偎取暖的溫馨港灣，此時已經被遠遠拋在身後了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Her home, the destination she yearned for where she could share warmth with her mother, lay deeply behind her.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 渴望、熱望（過去式，後常接 to be 或 for）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 遠遠落在後面。

grammarNotes.structure: where she longed to be, where she and her mother...
grammarNotes.explanation: 連續兩個關係副詞 where 引導的非限制性定語從句，修飾前面的名詞 Home。

sentences.englishOriginal: It's got to be closer to the palace, she thought. But where any place was in all this snow, she couldn't be sure.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「現在這裡離皇宮應該更近一點才對，」她心想。但在這白茫茫鋪天蓋地的暴風雪中，她根本分不清東南西北，完全無法確定任何地方的位置。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'The palace must be nearer by now,' she reflected. However, surrounded by this blinding blizzard, she was completely disoriented.

phraseNotes.explanation: 離……更近。
確定、確信。

grammarNotes.explanation: 由 where 引導的間接問句，作為整個句子的前置主題（倒裝/提前加強語氣）。

sentences.englishOriginal: She plowed on, dragging furrows with her sore foot. The short winter day was almost done.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她極其艱難地在厚厚的雪地中破雪前行，受傷的腳在雪面上拖出一道長長的深溝。而冬日那短暫的白晝此時已悄然接近尾聲。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She forced her way forward, carving deep tracks in the snow with her injured foot as the brief winter day drew to a close.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 艱難地破雪/破浪前行、堅持不懈地往前（過去式）。
犁溝、溝壑（複數）。此處生動形容腳在深雪中拖行留下的深痕。

phraseNotes.explanation: 幾乎結束了、差不多完成了。

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在分詞短語作伴隨狀語，說明她前進時的姿態與留下的痕跡。

sentences.englishOriginal: Am I still going the right way? she wondered. There was no one around to advise her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我走的路到底對不對？」她暗自納悶。然而這天寒地凍之中，四周連個鬼影子都沒有，根本沒有人能給她指引方向。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Am I moving in the correct direction?' she asked herself, with absolutely nobody nearby to guide her.

phraseNotes.explanation: 正確的方向、正確的路徑。

sentences.englishOriginal: Whoever else there was in this snow-covered world was far, far away, and safe indoors-- even the animals in their burrows. She went plodding on.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在這個被冰雪覆蓋的世界裡，不論還有誰活著，肯定都躲在遠在天邊的溫暖室內安穩度日——就連洞穴裡的小動物也不例外。只有她，依舊在雪地中孤獨而沉重地一步步跋涉。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Any other living soul in this white landscape was miles away, sheltered warm inside—even the wildlife in their dens. Yet she kept dragging herself forward.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: （野兔、狐狸等挖掘的）地下洞穴（複數）。
沉重而緩慢地行走、埋頭苦幹。

phraseNotes.explanation: 安全地待在室內。

grammarNotes.explanation: whoever 引導的讓步/名詞從句，作為整個句子的主詞，意為「不論還有其他任何人」。

sentences.englishOriginal: Soon night took over. She knew in the dark that the muffled snow was still falling-- she could feel it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不久，黑夜徹底籠罩了大地。即便置身於伸手不見五指的黑暗中，她也清楚地知道那無聲無息的厚雪仍在不斷飄落——因為她能切實感受到那落在身上的冰冷與沉重。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Shortly, darkness consumed the environment. In the pitch black, she was fully aware that the silent snow was continuing to fall, as she could sense it on her skin.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 裹住的、低沉的。此處作文學修辭，形容大雪覆蓋大地後那種萬籟俱寂、聲音被隔絕的「沉寂之雪」。

phraseNotes.explanation: 黑夜接管了世界、夜幕降臨。

sentences.englishOriginal: She was cold and alone in the middle of nowhere. Irene was lost.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她在這個荒無人煙、前不著村後不著店的地方捱著凍，孤立無援。艾琳徹底迷路了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Freezing and isolated, she found herself stranded in an unfamiliar void. Irene had completely lost her way.

phraseNotes.explanation: 非常道地的英文片語，形容「在荒郊野外、在前不著村後不著店的荒涼孤立之地」。

sentences.englishOriginal: She had to keep moving. She was hoping she'd come to a house, any house at all, and be taken in.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她明白自己必須不停地走下去。她多麼由衷地期盼自己能迎面撞見一棟房子，不管是誰的家都好，只要能好心收留她進去躲躲風雪。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She understood that halting meant danger, so she forced herself to walk, praying to encounter any dwelling that might offer her temporary shelter.

phraseNotes.explanation: 持續移動、不停下腳步。
被收留、被接納（此處為被動語態）。在其他語境下也有「受騙上當」的意思，需注意。

sentences.englishOriginal: She badly needed to be in someone's arms. The snow was above her knees now.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 此時此刻，她無比渴望能被疼愛地緊抱在溫暖的懷抱裡。而此時，地上的積雪已經深得沒過了她的膝蓋。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She desperately yearned for a comforting embrace. By this time, the snowbanks had risen past her knees.

phraseNotes.explanation: 迫切、非常需要。

sentences.englishOriginal: She shoved her way through it, clutching the empty box.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她死死抱著那口空箱子，用盡全身力氣在沒膝的深雪中奮力撥開一條血路前行。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She forced her upper body against the drifts, holding tightly onto the barren package.

phraseNotes.explanation: 用力推開、在阻礙中奮力擠出一條前進的路。

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在分詞短語作伴隨狀語，修飾主句的動作細節。

stories.title: The Golden Sled and The Palace Miracle

sentences.englishOriginal: She was asking how long a small person could keep this struggle up, when she realized it was getting lighter.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她正絕望地自問一個如此弱小的孩子究竟還能苦苦撐到什麼時候，就在這時，她突然驚覺周圍似乎變得明亮了起來。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Just as she was questioning how much longer a small child could endure this grueling ordeal, she suddenly noticed the darkness beginning to lift.

phraseNotes.explanation: 將這場抗爭/奮鬥繼續堅持下去。

grammarNotes.structure: She was asking... when she realized...
grammarNotes.explanation: was/were doing... when... 經典句型，意為「正當正在做某事時，突然發生了另一件事」。

sentences.englishOriginal: There was a soft glow coming from somewhere below her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在她的正下方深處，正隱隱約約透出一股溫柔、和煦的光芒。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: A gentle illumination emanated from a location situated beneath her current position.

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在分詞短語後置修飾名詞 a soft glow。

sentences.englishOriginal: Waded toward this glow, and soon was gazing down a long slope at a brightly lit mansion.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她步履維艱地朝那道光芒走去，很快地，她便站在高處，俯瞰著長長山坡下一棟燈火通明、璀璨無比的宏偉豪宅。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She struggled through the snow toward the light, and shortly found herself looking down an extensive decline at a brilliantly illuminated estate.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 涉水、在此指在厚厚的積雪中「艱難地涉雪前進」（過去式）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 燈火輝煌的、光線充足的。

sentences.englishOriginal: It had to be the palace!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那一定就是皇宮了！
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: That location absolutely had to be the royal palace!

phraseNotes.explanation: 在此表肯定的強烈推測，「一定是、絕對是」。

sentences.englishOriginal: Irene pushed forward with all her strength and-- sloosh! thwump!-- she plunged downward and was buried.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳鼓起全身最後的一丁點力氣猛地往前一衝，結果——呼溜！撲通！——她整個人瞬間一腳踩空墜落下去，被沒頂的大雪徹底活埋了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Gathering every ounce of her remaining vitality, Irene surged ahead, but with a sudden slide and a thud, she dropped straight into a drift and vanished.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 縱身投入、猛烈墜落（過去式）。
擬聲詞。分別模擬滑倒的呼溜聲與重物落入雪堆中悶沉的「撲通」撞擊聲。

phraseNotes.explanation: 用盡全力。

sentences.englishOriginal: She had fallen off a little cliff.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 原來，她剛才是在不知情的情況下，不小心跌落了一處隱藏在雪中的矮斷崖。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Unwittingly, she had stepped over the edge of a small precipice concealed by the snow.

phraseNotes.explanation: 從……跌落。

grammarNotes.explanation: 過去完成式，表示在被活埋（plunged）之前就已經發生的原因動作。

sentences.englishOriginal: Only her hat and the box in her hands stuck out above the snow.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 此時地表上，只剩下她戴的那頂紅帽子和她死死攥在手裡的箱子還孤零零地露在雪面之外。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Nothing remained visible except for her winter hat and the container she held, protruding from the white surface.

phraseNotes.explanation: 突出、顯露出來（stick out 的過去式）。

sentences.englishOriginal: Even if she could call for help, no one would hear her. Her body shook. Her teeth chattered.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 哪怕此時她還有力氣大聲呼救，這種鬼天氣也絕對不可能有人聽得見。她渾身劇烈顫抖，牙齒更是凍得不停咯咯作響。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Even on the off chance that she could shout for assistance, her voice would be lost to the storm. Her entire frame vibrated, and her teeth clicked from the cold.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: （因寒冷或恐懼而牙齒）咯咯作響、嘮叨（過去式）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 呼救。

grammarNotes.structure: Even if she could..., no one would...
grammarNotes.explanation: 典型的與過去/現在事實相反的虛擬語氣（Conditional Sentence），展示極度絕望的情境。

sentences.englishOriginal: Why not freeze to death, she thought, and let all these troubles end. Why not? She was already buried.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「要不乾脆就這樣凍死算了，」她自暴自棄地心想，「好讓這一切痛苦和折磨都徹底解脫。有何不可呢？反正我橫豎都已經被活埋了。」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Perhaps it is best to simply freeze to death,' she reflected gloomily, 'and bring a permanent conclusion to all this misery. Why fight it? I am already entombed.'

phraseNotes.explanation: 凍死。
有何不可？為什麼不呢？

sentences.englishOriginal: And never see her mother's face again? Her good mother who smelled like fresh-baked bread?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但是，難道這輩子就再也見不到媽媽的面容了嗎？她那無比溫柔、身上總是散發著剛出爐新鮮麵包香氣的好媽媽啊！
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: But could she accept never gazing upon her mother's countenance again? That wonderful woman who constantly carried the aroma of freshly baked loaves?

phraseNotes.explanation: 聞起來像……

grammarNotes.explanation: 關係代名詞 who 引導的定語從句，修飾前面的 Her good mother。

sentences.englishOriginal: In an explosion of fury, she flung her body about to free herself and was finally able to climb up on her knees and look around.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 想到這裡，一股排山倒海的憤怒與不甘瞬間在她體內炸裂開來，她瘋狂地扭動拍打著肢體試圖掙脫積雪，最終，她成功地用雙膝跪爬了出來，並環顧四周。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Fueled by a sudden outburst of fierce resolve, she thrashed her limbs violently to liberate herself, eventually succeeding in rising onto her knees to survey her surroundings.

phraseNotes.explanation: 讓自己獲得自由、掙脫束縛。
環顧四周。

sentences.englishOriginal: How to get down to that glittering palace? As soon as she raised the question, she had the answer.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 該怎麼從這陡峭的地方下到那座金碧輝煌的皇宮呢？這個問題才剛在她腦海中浮現，她便福至心靈地有了絕妙的答案。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: By what means could she descend to that shimmering residence? The very instant the dilemma crossed her mind, an ingenious solution presented itself.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 閃閃發光的、耀眼奪目的。

phraseNotes.explanation: 一……就……（引導時間副詞從句）。
提出問題、引發思考。

grammarNotes.explanation: 疑問詞 + 不定式短語，常在口語或內心獨白中獨立作句子使用。

sentences.englishOriginal: She laid the box down and climbed aboard.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她毫不猶豫地把那個大箱子橫著平放在雪地上，接著自己整個人跨坐了上去。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She positioned the large container flat onto the drift and mounted it like a vehicle.

phraseNotes.explanation: 把某物放下、平放（lay 的過去式）。
登上（交通工具、火車、船或此處充當雪橇的箱子）。

sentences.englishOriginal: But it pressed into the snow and stuck.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然而，箱子卻深深陷進了鬆軟的雪中，卡得死死的，一動也不動。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: However, the cardboard sank heavily into the powder and remained immobilized.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 卡住、陷入、無法移動（stick 的過去式）。

sentences.englishOriginal: She tried again, and this time, instead of climbing on, she leaped. The box shot forward, like a sled.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她毫不氣餒再試了一次，而這一次，她不再是慢慢爬上去，而是看準時機縱身猛地一跳！箱子受到這股衝力，頓時像一輛脫韁的雪橇般朝前方瘋狂飛馳而去！
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Attempting a different approach, she vaulted onto it with a running leap instead of shifting weight slowly. Instantaneously, the box propelled forward exactly like a toboggan.

phraseNotes.explanation: 代替、而不是……（後接動名詞或名詞）。
箭一般地向前衝（shoot forward 的過去式）。

sentences.englishOriginal: The wind raced after Irene but couldn't keep up. In a moment she would be with people again, inside where it was warm.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 暴風在艾琳身後沒命地狂追，卻怎麼也趕不上這瘋狂的「雪橇」。轉眼間，她就要重新回到人群之中、回到那個溫暖如春的室內了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The gale chased after her with all its velocity but proved incapable of matching her pace. In mere moments, she would be surrounded by human company within a heated sanctuary.

phraseNotes.explanation: 跟上、趕上、保持不落後。
轉眼間、立刻、一會兒。

sentences.englishOriginal: The sled slowed and jerked to a stop on paving stones.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「雪橇」的速度逐漸慢了下來，最後在宮殿前鋪設整齊的鋪路石磚上猛地頓了一下，穩穩地停了下來。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The makeshift toboggan decelerated and came to an abrupt halt upon encountering the cobblestone courtyard.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: （使）猝然一動、猛地一頓（過去式）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 突然急停、猛然頓住停下。

sentences.englishOriginal: The time had come to break the bad news to the duchess.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 現在，最艱難的時刻到了——她必須進去把這個糟糕透頂的噩耗親口告訴公爵夫人了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The inevitable moment had arrived for her to convey the disappointing updates to the royal lady.

phraseNotes.explanation: ……的時刻終於到了。
透露消息（通常指委婉或沉重地宣布一個突如其來的消息，多用於壞消息）。

sentences.englishOriginal: With the empty box clasped to her chest, Irene strode nervously toward the palace.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳將那口空箱子死死緊抱在胸前，懷著無比忐忑惶恐的心情，硬著頭皮朝皇宮大門大步走去。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Clutching the hollow parcel securely against her torso, Irene advanced with great trepidation toward the entrance.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 緊抱、緊握（過去式或過去分詞）。
大跨步走（stride 的過去式）。

grammarNotes.structure: with the empty box clasped to her chest
grammarNotes.explanation: with + 受詞 + 受詞補語（過去分詞）的複合結構，表示伴隨的狀態。

sentences.englishOriginal: But then her feet stopped moving and her mouth fell open. She stared.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然而就在此時，她的雙腳像是被施了定身法般突然定住，小嘴更是震驚得張得老大。她整個人不可置信地愣愣凝視著前方。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Suddenly, however, her lower limbs froze, and her jaw dropped in absolute disbelief as she fixated her gaze on something nearby.

phraseNotes.explanation: 目瞪口呆、驚訝得張大嘴巴（fall open 的過去式）。

sentences.englishOriginal: Maybe this was impossible, yet there it was, a little way off and over to the right, hugging the trunk of a tree--
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這聽起來簡直是天方夜譚、根本不可能發生的神蹟，但它就活生生地呈現在眼前！就在不遠處的右前方，緊緊死纏在一棵大樹的樹幹上——
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Though it defied all logic, the object was undeniably present a short distance away toward the right, wrapped tightly around a tree trunk—

phraseNotes.explanation: 在不遠處。
緊緊抱住樹幹（擬人化，形容衣服死死纏繞在樹上）。

sentences.englishOriginal: The beautiful ball gown! The wind was holding it there.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 竟然是那件美麗無雙的舞會禮服！淘氣的風此時就像一個忠誠的衛兵一樣，用氣流將它牢牢地按在樹幹上保管著呢！
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: It was the magnificent evening dress! The selfsame wind had pinned it against the bark.

sentences.englishOriginal: Mama! Irene shouted. Mama, I found it!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「媽媽！」艾琳興奮得大喊大叫。「媽媽！我找到了！」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Mommy!' Irene vociferated joyously. 'Mommy, I actually recovered it!'

sentences.englishOriginal: She managed somehow, despite the wind's meddling, to get the gown off the tree and back in its box.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 儘管那陣風依然在旁邊調皮地百般刁難阻撓，她仍舊不可思議地設法將禮服完好無損地從小樹上取了下來，並重新整整齊齊地塞回了箱子裡。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Through persistent effort and notwithstanding the gale's constant interference, she succeeded in detaching the garment and placing it back inside the carton.

phraseNotes.explanation: 儘管、不管（後接名詞或代名詞）。
把某物從……上面取下來。

sentences.englishOriginal: And in another moment she was at the door of the palace. She knocked twice with the big brass knocker.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 下一秒，她便已經健步如飛地站在皇宮宏偉的大門前。她抓起沉重的純銅大門閂，用力扣扣敲了兩下大門。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Within seconds, she presented herself at the palace gates, utilizing the massive bronze hardware to deliver two firm raps.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 門環、門閂（用來敲門敲擊出聲的裝置）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 馬上、緊接著。

stories.title: A Radiant Ball and The Triumphant Return

sentences.englishOriginal: The door opened and she burst in.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 大門霍地一聲打開了，她像一陣風似地猛地衝了進去。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The massive entrance swung wide, and she rushed inside without hesitation.

sentences.englishOriginal: She was welcomed by cheering servants and a delirious duchess.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 映入眼簾的是夾道歡迎、大聲歡呼的僕人們，以及高興得簡直要失去理智、欣喜若狂的公爵夫人。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She received an ecstatic reception from shouting house staff and an incredibly ecstatic duchess.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 精神錯亂的、在此形容「高興到發狂的、欣喜若狂的」。

grammarNotes.explanation: 標準被動語態，強調小女孩一進門所受到的熱烈待遇。

sentences.englishOriginal: They couldn't believe she had come over the mountain in such a storm, all by herself.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 所有人都不敢置信，這個弱小的女孩子竟然能在如此恐怖的狂風暴雪中，單槍匹馬一個人翻山越嶺把東西送來。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The entire court found it incredible that she had successfully navigated the mountain pass completely unassisted during such a blizzard.

phraseNotes.explanation: 完全靠她自己、獨自一人。
越過、過來。

grammarNotes.explanation: believe 後接賓語從句；從句中使用過去完成式 had come，因為「翻山越嶺」發生在「不敢置信（couldn't believe）」之前。

sentences.englishOriginal: She had to tell the whole story, every detail. And she did.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 大家都圍著她，要她把這一路上的驚險過程從頭到尾、不放過任何細節地講一遍。而她也毫無保留地娓娓道來。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: They insisted she recount the entire narrative down to the smallest detail, which she promptly accomplished.

sentences.englishOriginal: Then she asked to be taken right back to her sick mother.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 講完故事後，她迫不及待地懇求大家立刻送她回家，回到她那正臥病在床的媽媽身邊。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Subsequently, she requested immediate transportation back to her ailing parent.

phraseNotes.explanation: 立刻回去。
請求被……（被動不定式）。

sentences.englishOriginal: But it was out of the question, they said; the road that ran around the mountain wouldn't be cleared until morning.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但眾人異口同聲地說，這在目前是絕對不可能的；因為環山公路積雪太厚，最快也必須等到明天下天亮才有辦法完全清空通車。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: However, they maintained that such a trip was totally impossible, as the mountain highway would remain blocked by snow until dawn.

phraseNotes.explanation: 非常經典且必學的片語，意思是「絕不可能、毫無商量餘地」（注意不要和 out of question「毫無疑問」搞混）。

grammarNotes.structure: the road [that ran around the mountain]
grammarNotes.explanation: 關係代名詞 that 引導的定語從句，修飾主詞 the road。

sentences.englishOriginal: Don't fret, child, said the duchess. Your mother is surely sleeping now. We'll get you there first thing tomorrow.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「別擔心，好孩子，」公爵夫人溫柔地安慰道。「你媽媽現在肯定已經安穩地睡著了。明天一早，我向你保證第一件事就是親自派車送你回家。」
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Do not worry, my dear,' comforted the duchess. 'Your mother is undoubtedly resting comfortably at this hour. We shall ensure you return home at the earliest light tomorrow.'

phraseNotes.explanation: 明天一大早、明天天亮後的第一件事。

sentences.englishOriginal: Irene was given a good dinner as she sat by the fire, the moisture steaming off her clothes.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳被安頓在溫暖的壁爐旁，僕人為她端來了豐盛的晚餐，大火烘烤著她濕透的衣裳，衣服上不斷冒出白色的水氣。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Irene enjoyed a hearty meal while seated adjacent to the hearth, as the residual dampness evaporated from her garments in columns of steam.

phraseNotes.explanation: 冒著水氣蒸發掉。

grammarNotes.explanation: 雙賓語動詞的被動語態（give someone something 改為 someone was given something）。
獨立主格結構（Absolute Structure），名詞 + 現在分詞，用來補充說明主句動作發生時的伴隨狀態。

sentences.englishOriginal: The duchess, meanwhile, got into her freshly ironed gown before the guests began arriving in their sleighs.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 與此同時，公爵夫人趕在貴賓們陸續乘坐雪橇抵達之前，優雅地換上了那件剛剛被熨燙得平整如新的華美禮服。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: In the meantime, the noble lady dressed herself in the immaculately pressed garment prior to the arrival of the attendees in their cutters.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 熨燙過的（過去分詞作形容詞）。

sentences.englishOriginal: What a wonderful ball it was! The duchess in her new gown was like a bright star in the sky.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那真是一場美輪美奐、令人終生難忘的盛大舞會啊！身著新禮服的公爵夫人此時就像夜空中最耀眼奪目的一顆明星。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The evening turned out to be an absolutely magnificent celebration! The duchess, adorned in her tailored attire, resembled a radiant star in the firmament.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 容光煥發的、光芒四射的。

phraseNotes.phrase: What a + Adj + Noun + it was!
phraseNotes.explanation: 感嘆句型，「多麼……的一個……啊！」

sentences.englishOriginal: Irene in her ordinary dress was radiant.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而艾琳儘管只穿著她那身普普通通的常服，此時卻也顯得如此明艷動人、光彩照人。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Even while wearing her modest everyday clothes, Irene looked completely beautiful and glowing.

sentences.englishOriginal: She was swept up into dances by handsome aristocrats, who kept her feet off the floor to spare her ankle.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那些英俊的貴族們紛紛熱情地邀請她共舞，甚至體貼地在跳舞時將她整個人輕輕托起離開地面，好細心呵護她那隻受傷扭痛的腳踝。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She was enthusiastically escorted onto the dance floor by attractive noblemen, who thoughtfully lifted her during the movements to protect her sprained limb.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 免除、饒恕、在此指「保護、免受進一步傷害/心疼」。

phraseNotes.explanation: 一下子被捲入、著迷地投入到某個氛圍或活動中（過去式被動語態）。

grammarNotes.explanation: 關係代名詞 who 引導的非限制性定語從句，對 aristocrats 進行補充說明。

sentences.englishOriginal: Her mother would enjoy hearing all about it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 艾琳心裡甜滋滋地想，媽媽回家聽她講起這一切時，一定會高興壞的。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Her parent would undoubtedly delight in listening to the comprehensive narrative of these events.

phraseNotes.explanation: 聽聞關於這件事的點點滴滴/所有細節。

grammarNotes.explanation: enjoy 後面必須接動名詞（V-ing）作為受詞。

sentences.englishOriginal: Early the next morning, when snow had long since ceased falling, Mrs. Bobbin woke from a good night's sleep feeling much improved.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 隔天清晨，大雪早就已經停了，巴賓太太經過一夜的香甜安穩睡眠後醒來，驚喜地發現自己的身體狀態已經好轉了許多。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: At dawn the following day, long after the storm had finished dispersing, Mrs. Bobbin awakened from a restful slumber feeling significantly healthier.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 停止、結束（過去式，文學色彩較濃的用詞）。

phraseNotes.explanation: 早就、很久以前就……
從……中醒來（wake 的過去式）。

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在分詞短語作伴隨狀語/主詞補語，描述主詞醒來時的精神與身體狀態。

sentences.englishOriginal: She hurried about and got a fire going in the cold stove. Then she went to look in on Irene.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她急急忙忙下了床，在冰冷的爐子裡重新生起了火。接著，她輕手輕腳地走到隔壁，想看看艾琳睡得怎麼樣了。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: She moved briskly to ignite a blaze within the freezing hearth. Afterward, she proceeded to check on Irene's condition.

phraseNotes.explanation: 把火生起來、使爐火燃燒。
極其道地的片語，意思是「順道看望、短時間內探視/查看某人（特別是病人、小孩或睡覺中的人）的情況」。

sentences.englishOriginal: But Irene's bed was empty! She ran to the window and gazed at the white landscape.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然而，艾琳的床鋪竟然空空如也！她大驚失色，一個箭步衝到窗前，焦急地望向外面那白茫茫的雪景。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: To her horror, Irene's mattress was unoccupied! She rushed to the glass and stared out at the blanketed terrain.

sentences.englishOriginal: No one out there. Snow powder fell from the branch of a tree.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 外面空無一人，死寂一片。只有一星半點細碎的雪粉，偶爾從乾枯的樹枝上輕輕飄落下來。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Not a single soul was visible outside. Only a trace of crystalline powder drifted down from a nearby limb.

sentences.englishOriginal: Where is my child? Mrs. Bobbin cried. She whipped on her coat to go out and find her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我的天啊，我的孩子上哪兒去了？！」巴賓太太急得大哭起來。她一把扯過大衣胡亂套在身上，作勢就要衝出門去找人。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: 'Where has my baby gone?' Mrs. Bobbin shrieked in panic. She threw on her winter jacket, determined to venture out and locate her daughter.

phraseNotes.explanation: 極其生動口語的說法，意思是「啪一聲迅速穿上、以極快的速度套上（衣服等）」。

sentences.englishOriginal: When she pulled the door open, a wall of drift faced her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 可當她一使勁拉開大門時，一整面被狂風吹來堆積而成的、高聳如牆壁般的巨大雪垛，結結實實地堵在了她面前。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: The instant she unlocked and swung the door open, a massive barrier of accumulated snow blocked her exit entirely.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: 在此特指被風吹拂堆積在一起的「雪堆、雪垛」（snowdrift）。

sentences.englishOriginal: But peering over it, she could see a horse-drawn sleigh hastening up the path.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但她踮起腳尖越過雪牆努力往前看去，赫然發現一輛由高頭大馬拉著的奢華雪橇，正朝著她家的小路一路疾馳而來。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: However, squinting across the top of the drift, she detected a grand horse-drawn cutter accelerating along the trail.

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在分詞短語作伴隨狀語，修飾主句主詞 she 的動作。
感官動詞 see 的經典用法：see + 受詞 + 現在分詞（V-ing），強調動作正在發生或該動作的動態感。

sentences.englishOriginal: And seated on the sleigh, between two large footmen, was Irene herself, asleep but smiling.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而在那輛雪橇的座位中央，正緊緊依偎在兩名身材魁梧、穿著體面的皇家男僕中間的，不是別人，正是艾琳自己！她此時正甜甜地睡著，嘴角還掛著一抹幸福的微笑。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: And positioned within the carriage, flanked by two burly royal attendants, sat Irene herself, lost in slumber yet wearing a joyful expression.

vocabularyNotes.explanation: （舊時的）男僕、隨從（複數，單數為 footman）。

grammarNotes.structure: And seated on the sleigh... was Irene herself...
grammarNotes.explanation: 完全倒裝句型（Full Inversion）。為了將真正的主詞 Irene herself 放在後面進行焦點強調，將過去分詞短語與地點介系詞短語提前。

sentences.englishOriginal: Would you like to hear the rest?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你們還想聽聽故事接下來的尾聲嗎？
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: Would you care to listen to the concluding details of the story?

phraseNotes.explanation: 剩餘的部分、其餘的事物。

sentences.englishOriginal: Well, there was a bearded doctor in the back of the sleigh...
sentences.chineseTranslation: 嗯，在雪橇的後座上，其實還坐著一位留著大鬍子的皇家醫生呢……（那是公爵夫人特地派來給媽媽治病的）。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: To give you the facts, a whiskered physician was traveling in the rear of the carriage...

sentences.englishOriginal: and the duchess had sent Irene's mother a ginger cake with white icing, some oranges and a pineapple, and spice candy of many flavors,
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不僅如此，公爵夫人還貼心地給艾琳的媽媽送來了一整塊鋪滿白色糖霜的薑餅蛋糕、滿滿一籃柳橙和鳳梨，以及各式各樣不同口味的風味香料糖果，
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: furthermore, the noblewoman had dispatched a frosted gingerbread confection, a selection of citrus fruit and a pineapple, along with spiced sweets of diverse varieties for Irene's parent,

phraseNotes.explanation: 薑蛋糕/薑餅。在西方文化中，冬天吃薑製糕點有著驅寒、滋補與節慶的溫馨寓意。

grammarNotes.structure: had sent Irene's mother a ginger cake...
grammarNotes.explanation: 過去完成式搭配雙賓語動詞（send someone something），表示派送禮物這件事發生在回到家之前。

sentences.englishOriginal: along with a note saying how much she cherished her gown, and what a brave and loving person Irene was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 隨禮物還附帶了一封親筆親手寫的感謝信，信中由衷地讚美了她有多麼珍愛這件精美絕倫的禮服，更高度讚賞了艾琳是一個多麼勇敢無畏、多麼體貼孝順的完美孩子。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: accompanied by a personal letter expressing how deeply she valued her new attire, and describing what an incredibly courageous and affectionate individual Irene had proven to be.

phraseNotes.explanation: 伴隨著、連同……一起。

phraseNotes.phrase: what a + Adj + N + Subj + Verb
phraseNotes.explanation: 感嘆句結構（what a brave... person Irene was!）。

grammarNotes.explanation: 現在分詞短語後置修飾名詞 a note，意為「一封寫著……內容的信」（say 在此作「寫道、內容是」解）。

grammarNotes.structure: saying how much... and what a...
grammarNotes.explanation: saying 後面接了兩個由 how 和 what 引導的賓語從句作為其內容受詞。

sentences.englishOriginal: Which, of course, Mrs. Bobbin knew. Better than the duchess.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而這些美好的品質，做母親的巴賓太太當然早就知道了。而且，她絕對比公爵夫人還要更加了解、更加引以為傲呢。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: All of which, naturally, Mrs. Bobbin was already fully aware of—far better than the noble lady could ever comprehend.

phraseNotes.explanation: 當然、自然而然。
比……更清楚/更好。

grammarNotes.structure: Which, of course, Mrs. Bobbin knew.
grammarNotes.explanation: 非限制性關係代名詞 Which 提前引導的獨立短句（文學修辭手法的非正式斷句），指代前文公爵夫人對艾琳的讚美。

sentences.englishOriginal: I hope you enjoyed this book, 'Brave Irene.'
sentences.chineseTranslation: 希望你們都會喜歡這本精彩的圖畫書——《勇敢的艾琳》。
sentences.aiEnglishSuggestion: I truly hope you had a wonderful time listening to this volume, 'Brave Irene.'
